title: Oil Price Hits Wartime High Above $120 a Barrel as Iran War Standoff Continues
summary: The longer the disruption to Middle East fuel supplies lasts, the risk grows that higher energy costs will feed into broader inflation that could dent economic growth.

title: Why the U.A.E. is Quitting OPEC
summary: The United Arab Emirates is walking away from OPEC this May. The New York Times’ energy reporter, Rebecca Elliott, breaks down how the war with Iran provided the perfect opening for the U.A.E. to go solo.

title: Iranians Live With Pain and Powerlessness, Beneath a Smooth Veneer
summary: After months of upheaval, many are attempting to get on with their lives while quietly grappling with grief, economic stress and a loss of hope.

title: Rising Fuel Prices Force Policymakers to Weigh Excruciating Choices
summary: The Bank of England and European Central Bank held interest rates steady on Thursday, as officials search for signs of possible longer-term damage and warn of the impact of a prolonged energy shock.

title: What the Voting Rights Act Decision Means, and Hegseth’s Heated Testimony
summary: Plus, chatbots told scientists how to make bioweapons.

title: In Blow to Voting Rights Act, Supreme Court Embraces Claim of Racial Progress
summary: The majority said the law was a victim of its own success and no longer needed. Dissenters responded that Congress should make the call.

title: Some Black Southerners Say Voting Rights Ruling ‘Missed the Mark’
summary: Veterans of the civil rights movement and others said the Supreme Court decision felt like a bleak end to decades of gains in Black representation in the region.

title: Democrats Regret Creating Independent Redistricting Commissions After Supreme Court Ruling
summary: A decade ago, the party sought independent redistricting commissions. Now, in an era of extreme gerrymandering, such efforts could slow Democrats as they try to keep up with Republicans.

title: U.S. Economy Grew 2 Percent in Early 2026 Even as War in Iran Began to Hit Energy Prices
summary: Gross domestic product expanded at a 2 percent annual rate in the first three months of the year, a period that included the first weeks of conflict in the Middle East.

title: Fed Meeting Underscores Tough Task Ahead for Warsh
summary: Jerome H. Powell on Wednesday announced he would stay on as a governor at the central bank as internal divisions sharpen about the policy path forward.

title: How Powell Just Complicated Trump’s Fed Plans
summary: Traders now think the Federal Reserve will keep steady interest rates, or perhaps even increase them, amid succession intrigue at the central bank.

title: Attacks on Jewish Targets in Europe Suggest Hybrid Warfare
summary: Officials are investigating similar attacks across Europe, all claimed by a shadowy Islamist group that may be using low-cost, unsophisticated methods to sow fear in Jewish communities.

title: Police in Australia Were Warned of Terror Risk Before Bondi Attack, Report Says
summary: A Jewish security group told police an attack on the community was “likely” because of heightened antisemitism, days before December’s mass shooting in Sydney.

title: Mariah Explains Her Hit-Producing Songwriting Process
summary: The artist talks about writing her era-defining hits, the pleasures of collaboration and some of the ways she has been undercredited as a writer.

title: How Taylor Swift Writes a Song, In Her Own Words
summary: The artist shares stories behind some of her biggest hits, her love of a “rant bridge” and how life in the public eye informs the stories she tells in her songs.

title: British Royals Crisscross Manhattan in Brief Visit Packed With Photo Ops
summary: King Charles III and Queen Camilla laid flowers at the Sept. 11 memorial before stopping by an urban farm, the New York Public Library, a business event and a gala.

title: What the Royal State Dinner Guest List Says About Trump’s America
summary: There were at least 10 American billionaires, six Fox News hosts, assorted presidential pals, no Democratic politicians and not so many British.

title: Mamdani Suggested UK Should Return the Koh-i-Noor Diamond. Here’s the Back Story.
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York suggested that Britain should return the gem, a symbol of colonial plunder. Here’s the back story of the famed jewel.

title: In France, Trump’s Triumphal Arch Draws Eye Rolls
summary: The Arc de Triomphe in Paris, which the president vows to top in Washington, offers a lesson in the complicated history of monuments.

title: Trump May Pull Troops From Germany. That Isn’t as Scary as It Once Was.
summary: Germany has hugely increased its military spending, aiming to be less dependent on Washington. Its support for U.S. attacks on Iran may also give it leverage.

title: Ukraine Reacts With Shrug to Phone Call Between Trump and Putin
summary: More than a year of similar conversations have failed to bring the country any closer to peace, so Ukrainians have stopped hoping they will.

title: Minimal Comfort Feeding Is a New, Controversial Approach in Late Dementia
summary: Some consider the regular feeding of late-stage dementia patients to be nonnegotiable. Others see it as extending life unnecessarily.

title: J. Craig Venter, Scientist Who Decoded the Human Genome, Dies at 79
summary: A risk-taking outsider, he brought speed, competition and controversy to one of science’s biggest races.

title: High-Profile Squatting Incidents Are on the Rise Across the Country
summary: States are responding to a rise in high-profile squatting cases, in luxury homes and public housing alike.

title: The U.S. Military Was Losing Its Edge. After Iran, Everyone Knows It.
summary: Somehow, the weaker nation is in the stronger negotiating position.

title: Silicon Valley Is Bracing for a Permanent Underclass
summary: The people building A.I. fear that we have only a short time before advanced A.I. disrupts the labor force.

title: Who’s a Threat?
summary: We look at when political criticism can be considered a threat of violence.

title: Josh Johnson Gives King Charles Props for His Comedy Chops
summary: “You know, I’ve always wondered what white Def Jam would be like,” Johnson said after King Charles cracked a few jokes at the White House.

title: Janet Mills, Governor of Maine, Suspends Senate Campaign
summary: Her withdrawal reflected the strength of her primary opponent, Graham Platner, as well as the mixed recruitment record of Chuck Schumer, the minority leader, as his party tries to reclaim the Senate.

title: Forest Service Research Labs Are Closing
summary: A research lab in Washington State tracks ecological changes in a warming climate and provides scientific guidance for forest managers. It is one of 57 such facilities being shuttered.

title: David Allan Coe, Singer Who Personified Outlaw Country, Dies at 86
summary: Mr. Coe, who wrote “Take This Job and Shove It” and other chart-topping hits, was a transgressive exponent of the outlaw country movement of the 1970s and ’80s.

title: A Way to Slow Down ‘Super-Speeders’ Who Keep Breaking the Law
summary: City and state officials want speed-limiting devices installed in the cars of drivers who get more than 16 speeding tickets in a year, in hopes of preventing injuries and deaths.
